name: Spectre
summary: Repository indexing entry for the ``Spectre`` project. Index entry in the KFIOSA catalog. Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner. Attribution and provenance only; audit code, releases, and licence before use in the lab or production environment.

usage_sections.content: No CLI — install APK (GitHub Releases / F-Droid / Obtainium) on an Android 12+ device and launch the app. No Linux/Kali entrypoint.

examples.command: No CLI — install APK (GitHub Releases / F-Droid / Obtainium) on an Android 12+ device and launch the app. No Linux/Kali entrypoint.

dependencies: Android system APIs (WifiManager, TelephonyManager, BluetoothLeScanner, LocationManager/GNSS)
algorithms: Wideband RF power reconstruction: per-emitter dBm -> linear mW, sum, back to dBm; cellular normalized to wideband-equivalent via modem RSSI or bandwidth-derived offset
Distance estimation: true ranging via Wi-Fi 802.11mc FTM and cellular timing-advance where available, else path-loss model calibrated from iBeacon measured-power field, with confidence label
LAN host discovery via TCP-connect probes, SSDP/UPnP, mDNS/DNS-SD, banner grabbing + service fingerprinting + reverse DNS
BLE GATT inspection: connect, enumerate services/characteristics, read/decode values, write writable characteristics
Cellular monitoring: serving+neighbor cells across 5G/4G/3G/2G with operator/bands/RSRP/RSCP/RSSI + timing-advance distance, multi-SIM
GNSS tracking: multi-constellation, dual-frequency, C/N0, elevation/azimuth, used-in-fix, sub-satellite ground point
produces: Total RF exposure gauge (single summed dBm across cellular+Wi-Fi+BLE)
Per-cell: operator, identifiers, bands, RSRP/RSCP/RSSI, timing-advance distance
Per-AP: vendor, security, cipher, channel, width, RSSI, FTM or path-loss distance + confidence label
Per-BLE device: name, MAC, vendor, RSSI, address type, PHY, service UUIDs, decoded manufacturer/iBeacon data
Per-satellite: constellation, C/N0, elevation, azimuth, fix status, ground point
LAN: discovered hosts, banners, fingerprints, mDNS services
novelty: Not an airodump-ng/wash/hcxtools/scapy wrapper — it is a compiled Kotlin Android app using Android system APIs with no monitor mode, no root, no raw packet injection. Genuinely novel elements: (1) unified cellular+Wi-Fi+BLE+GNSS observation in one app, (2) physically-correct wideband RF-exposure reconstruction via linear power summation rather than naive dBm addition, (3) multi-SIM serving/neighbor-cell monitoring, (4) confidence-labeled distance estimates that distinguish true FTM/timing-advance ranging from path-loss models. None of this overlaps with KFIOSA's Linux pcap/monitor-mode WiFi pipeline; it cannot capture handshakes or do WPS/802.11 exploitation.

trust.warning: External community repo. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use. Catalogued for the AI's awareness; novel algorithms are implemented in core/modules/catalog_recon.py, not shelled out to this repo's binaries.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
